resources.string: Crimson lenses flicker beneath wet hides draped across metallic frames
RED LENSES FLICKER BENEATH WET SKIN ON METAL BONES.

The curse did not end at immortality. Some crawl toward the living with stolen hides dripping from their frames, desperate to imitate the warmth they cannot feel. Others have replaced limbs with heavy ordnance, and pity with the calculus of extinction. One envies every heartbeat. One hates the fact that heartbeats exist. Between them lies the final mercy of the dynasty: all organic life reduced to silence.
THE CURSE DID NOT END. SOME WEAR STOLEN SKIN. OTHERS REPLACE LIMBS WITH GUNS. ENVY AND HATRED AGREE THAT ALL ORGANIC LIFE MUST FALL SILENT.
